7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Last year, a friend of mine got laid off. Solid engineer, eight years of experience, good references, the kind of person who used to get recruiter messages every week without asking for them.
He told me, “Give me six weeks, max.”
It took him a little over five months.
And here’s the thing. He wasn’t doing anything wrong. He wasn’t lazy, he wasn’t picky, and his resume wasn’t broken. He was just job hunting in a market that quietly changed the rules while nobody was looking.
If you’ve been feeling like your search is taking forever, I want to show you the numbers that prove it’s not you. It’s the market. And then I want to walk through what actually changed, because “the market is bad” is not an explanation. It’s a shrug.
The number that should be on the news
Huntr, a job application tracker, publishes quarterly reports based on hundreds of thousands of real tracked applications from real job seekers. Not surveys where people guess. Actual timestamped data: when someone started searching, when they applied, when they interviewed, when they got an offer.
Here’s their median time from starting a job search to receiving a first offer:
